summary: In this paper abstract, Geoffrey Hinton and co-authors introduce 'knowledge distillation', a technique to compress the performance benefits of a large ensemble of models into a single, computationally efficient neural network. They also propose a novel ensemble architecture using 'specialist models' that target fine-grained classes which general models confuse.

principles.statement: Averaging predictions from multiple models trained on the same data is a reliable way to improve performance.
principles.rationale: It is a very simple method that improves almost any machine learning algorithm.
principles.source_quote: A very simple way to improve the performance of almost any machine learning algorithm is to train many different models on the same data and then to average their predictions.

principles.statement: The knowledge contained within a large, computationally expensive ensemble can be compressed into a single model.
principles.rationale: Ensembles are often too cumbersome for deployment, so compressing their knowledge allows for the performance benefits of an ensemble with the efficiency of a single model.
principles.source_quote: ...it is possible to compress the knowledge in an ensemble into a single model which is much easier to deploy...

frameworks.name: Knowledge Distillation
frameworks.when_to_apply: When an ensemble of models (especially large neural nets) is too computationally expensive or cumbersome to deploy to a large number of users.
steps: Train many different models on the same data to create an ensemble.
Compress the knowledge from the ensemble into a single model using distillation techniques.
Deploy the single, distilled model to users.
frameworks.source_quote: we show that we can significantly improve the acoustic model of a heavily used commercial system by distilling the knowledge in an ensemble of models into a single model.

mental_models.name: Full Models vs. Specialist Models
mental_models.description: An ensemble structure where general 'full models' are supplemented by 'specialist models'. The specialists are specifically trained to distinguish fine-grained classes that the full models confuse, allowing for rapid, parallel training unlike traditional mixture-of-experts.
mental_models.example: Using specialist models to distinguish fine-grained classes that the full models confuse, which can be trained rapidly and in parallel.

heuristics: To easily improve a machine learning algorithm's performance, train multiple models on the same data and average their predictions.

signature_quotes.text: A very simple way to improve the performance of almost any machine learning algorithm is to train many different models on the same data and then to average their predictions.
signature_quotes.context: Introducing the baseline benefit of ensemble learning before explaining the need for distillation.

anti_patterns.description: Deploying a whole ensemble of large neural networks directly to a large number of users.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Making predictions using a whole ensemble is cumbersome and computationally expensive.
